description: Defines how time works in this world — its structure and calendar. The spec distinguishes three time concepts: (1) the time system — the general concept of how time works; (2) time points — concrete identifiers for moments in time, attached to nodes and edges, whose format depends on the time system (e.g. "T0", "2024-03-15", "Cycle-3:Phase-2"); (3) frames — alternative timelines or branches where different nodes, edges, and constraints can be valid at the same time point.

type.description: The fundamental nature of time points in this world. LINEAR — time points have a natural order; "earlier" and "later" are defined. Time points are sorted alphabetically (lexicographic order), so authors must choose labels that sort correctly (e.g., "Day 01", "Day 02" instead of "Day 1", "Day 2"; or "2024-03-15", "2024-04-01"; or "T001", "T010"). ABSTRACT — time points are labels without inherent ordering; relationships between moments are defined through the graph. Useful for worlds where time is not a meaningful linear concept. Cyclical time and branching timelines are expressed through frames (loops via LOOPS_BACK relations, branches via parent + branches_at), not through the time system type.

calendar.description: Calendar structure — freely definable (months, cycles, seasons, etc.)

properties.note: Time has no direction here; connections define sequence
